political.s: Alphabet spent $20.6 million in political contributions and $14.8 million in lobbying during 2024, trending left with approximately 80% of donations to Democrats and 20% to Republicans. The company ramped up lobbying spending significantly during antitrust litigation with the DOJ.

charity.s: Google.org and the Google Foundation commit approximately $100 million annually to nonprofits focusing on education, economic opportunity, inclusion, and crisis response. The company has donated over $2 billion to nonprofits since 2017 and over $7 billion in ad grants.
charity.focus: Education, economic opportunity, inclusion, crisis response, climate

environment.s: Alphabet publishes comprehensive CDP climate reports, has committed to carbon neutrality, issued $5.75 billion in sustainability bonds, and scored 78/100 in independent sustainability assessment. However, rapid AI expansion raises concerns about energy consumption that offset sustainability gains.

labor.s: Alphabet faces multiple NLRB complaints for joint employer violations, anti-union activities including 'Project Vivian,' retaliation against organizing workers, and refusal to bargain with contract worker unions. Company unlawfully targeted employees engaged in organizing and surveilled labor activities.

dei.s: Google eliminated diversity hiring targets and removed DEI language from SEC filings in February 2025, citing changing legal landscape and Trump administration policies. Company dropped 50+ DEI-related organizations from funding lists and scaled back diversity grants.

animals.s: Not applicable - software and technology services company with no animal testing operations.

guns.s: Not applicable - technology and software services company does not manufacture or sell firearms.

privacy.s: Google faces significant privacy scrutiny for extensive data collection practices including location tracking even when disabled, behavior tracking across apps, and GDPR violations. Company has paid €50 million in GDPR fines and faces ongoing regulatory enforcement for deceptive consent and tracking practices.

execPay.s: CEO Sundar Pichai earned $10.7 million in 2024 compensation (32:1 ratio to median employee earning $331,894). Pichai received a prospective $692 million package in 2026 heavily dependent on Waymo and Wing performance metrics.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
